target.title: [Bug] Fix Feishu p2p DM replies fail with SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND (fixes #83730)
target.body:
## Summary

**What problem does this PR solve?**

Non-thread Feishu p2p (DM) replies are routed to `chat:<oc_xxx>` (the raw p2p chat ID) instead of `user:<open_id>`, causing `SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND` errors when the bot attempts to reply.

**Why does this matter now?**

Issue #83730 is a P1 regression introduced in OpenClaw 2026.5.12. After `skipReplyToInMessages` suppresses reply metadata for non-thread DMs, the visible reply becomes a top-level send to the `oc_*` chat target — which Feishu rejects.

**What is the intended outcome?**

- Non-thread p2p DM replies are sent to `user:<open_id>` instead of `chat:<oc_xxx>`
- Thread p2p DM replies and group replies are unaffected
- Streaming start, typing indicators, and conversation type detection keep using `chatId` as before

**What is intentionally out of scope?**

- No changes to other channels or the Feishu send/target resolution layer
- No changes to `send-target.ts`, `targets.ts`, or `send.ts`

**What does success look like?**

`sendMessageFeishu`, `sendMediaFeishu`, and `sendStructuredCardFeishu` receive `user:ou_*` as the `to:` target for non-thread p2p replies instead of `oc_*`.

**What should reviewers focus on?**

- `extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts` — `effectiveSendTarget` derivation, replacement of `to: chatId`, and streaming card delivery target
- `extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts` — passing `sendTarget: feishuTo` to both dispatcher calls

## Linked context

Closes #83730

Related clawtributors: #83764 (closed unmerged), #90636 (closed unmerged), #94761 (sibling PR, different approach)

Maintainer signal: ClawSweeper labeled `clawsweeper:queueable-fix`, `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`, `clawsweeper:source-repro`, `P1`, `impact:message-loss`

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Non-thread Feishu p2p DM replies use `user:<open_id>` target instead of raw `chatId`
- **Real environment tested:** Local checkout of openclaw fix branch `fix/issue-83730-feishu-p2p-dm-target` (commit 2adaa1f6ea) + live Feishu bot connected via WebSocket + Feishu app `cli_a9f668faaab89bd1`
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```
  # 1. Build OpenClaw from fix branch
  pnpm build

  # 2. Configure openclaw.json with Feishu app credentials (WebSocket mode)
  # 3. Start gateway with Feishu channel
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=~/feishu-test/openclaw.json \
    OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/feishu-test/data \
    pnpm openclaw gateway run --allow-unconfigured --auth none

  # 4. Send DM to bot via Feishu app
  # 5. Observe gateway logs
  ```
- **Evidence after fix — live Feishu gateway logs (redacted user ids):**

  ```
  2026-06-19T11:40:57.548+08:00 [feishu] feishu[default]: starting WebSocket connection...
  2026-06-19T11:40:57.553+08:00 [feishu] feishu[default]: WebSocket client started
  2026-06-19T11:40:57.546+08:00 [feishu] feishu[default]: bot open_id resolved: ou_<redacted>

  2026-06-19T11:43:19.255+08:00 [feishu] feishu[default]: received message from ou_<redacted> in oc_<redacted> (p2p)
  2026-06-19T11:43:19.271+08:00 [feishu] feishu[default]: Feishu[default] DM from ou_<redacted>: 你好你好
  2026-06-19T11:43:19.278+08:00 [feishu] feishu[default]: dispatching to agent (session=agent:main:main)

  2026-06-19T11:43:31.306+08:00 [feishu] feishu[default]: dispatch complete (queuedFinal=true, replies=1)
  ```

  Full 158-line gateway log available on request. No `SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND` entries in the entire log.

- **Evidence after fix — vitest:**
  ```
  ✓ reply-dispatcher.test.ts — 82 passed
  ✓ bot.test.ts — 87 passed
  ✓ channel.test.ts + send.test.ts + send-target.test.ts — 84 passed
  ```
- **Observed result after fix (live):** The Feishu bot received the DM and successfully dispatched a reply — no `SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND` error. The AI replied to the user in the Feishu chat with "嗨，你好啊。我在，有什么想让我帮你处理的吗？". A screenshot of the Feishu chat showing the bot reply is available.
- **What was not tested:** Group chat replies and thread DM replies (same code path, expected identical behavior). The bot app is missing `im:message.reactions:write_only` permission (needed for streaming interactive cards), so streaming cards fall back to non-streaming card delivery. This is a separate pre-existing permission issue that does not affect the dispatch target fix — the core reply delivery via `to: user:<open_id>` works correctly regardless.
- **Proof limitations:** The bot Feishu app lacks `contact:contact.base:readonly` permission (stale scope error, ignored) and `im:message.reactions:write_only` permission (streaming cards fall back to non-streaming delivery). Neither affects the p2p reply delivery fix. The live test was performed on a single Feishu user account; group and thread scenarios were not separately end-to-end tested.
- **Before evidence (optional):** On current main, `createFeishuReplyDispatcher` constructs all visible sends with `to: chatId` (the `oc_*` p2p chat ID), which Feishu rejects with `SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND` for non-thread DM replies.

## Tests and validation

- `CI=true node node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts` — 82 passed
- `CI=true node node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts` — 87 passed
- `CI=true node node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send-target.test.ts` — 84 passed
- All 253 tests green across the three test groups

**Regression coverage:** All existing Feishu reply-dispatcher and bot tests continue to pass. The fix is applied at the dispatcher parameter level — existing mocks verify the correct `to:` argument is used. Streaming card delivery now also routes through `effectiveSendTarget` with suppressed reply metadata.

## Risk checklist

**Did user-visible behavior change?** (`Yes`)

Yes — non-thread Feishu p2p DM replies now go to `user:<open_id>` instead of `chat:<oc_xxx>`, fixing the SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND error.

**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** (`No`)

**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** (`No`)

**What is the highest-risk area?** The `to:` target change affects all visible sends (text, card, media, no-visible-reply fallback). Streaming start, typing indicators, and conversation type detection are unchanged.

**How is that risk mitigated?** The `sendTarget` parameter defaults to `undefined`, falling back to `chatId` — zero behavior change for callers that don't pass it. Both dispatcher call sites in `bot.ts` pass the pre-computed `feishuTo` value, which equals `chat:<chatId>` for groups (identical behavior) and `user:<open_id>` for DMs (the fix). Live Feishu p2p DM test confirms `dispatch complete (queuedFinal=true, replies=1)` with no SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND.

## Current review state

**What is the next action?**

Maintainer / ClawSweeper re-review

**What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?**

- ClawSweeper re-review verdict
- CI checks (Real behavior proof gate with updated live evidence)
- Feishu p2p DM live test has been completed and evidence is included above
target.author: xydigit-zt
